    Methods in jellyfish that return Task
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Task
    TaskList.deleteTask(int index)
    Removes and returns the task at the given index.
    Task
    TaskList.getTask(int index)
    Returns the task at the given index.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ArrayList<Task>
    TaskList.getTasks()
    Returns the underlying ArrayList of tasks.
    Methods in jellyfish with parameters of type Task
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    TaskList.addTask(Task task)
    Adds a task to the list.
    static void
    Ui.showTaskAdded(Task task)
    Displays a confirmation that a task was added.
    static void
    Ui.showTaskDeleted(int position, Task task)
    Displays a confirmation that a task was deleted.
    static void
    Ui.showTaskMarked(int position, Task task)
    Displays a confirmation that a task was marked as done.
    static void
    Ui.showTaskUnmarked(int position, Task task)
    Displays a confirmation that a task was marked as not done.
    Method parameters in jellyfish with type arguments of type Task
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static void
    Storage.load(ArrayList<Task> tasks)
    Loads tasks from the data file into the given list.
    static void
    Storage.save(ArrayList<Task> tasks)
    Saves all tasks to the data file, overwriting any existing content.
    static void
    Ui.showFindResults(ArrayList<Task> results)
    Displays the results of a find command.
    static void
    Ui.showTaskList(ArrayList<Task> tasks)
    Displays all tasks in the list.
    Constructor parameters in jellyfish with type arguments of type Task
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    TaskList(ArrayList<Task> tasks)
    Creates a task list pre-populated with the given tasks.

  • Uses of Task in jellyfish.task

    Subclasses of Task in jellyfish.task
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Deadline
    Represents a task with a deadline.
    class 
    Event
    Represents a task with a start and end date or time.
    class 
    Todo
    Represents a todo task with no date or time attached.
